rulings.Case Title: 3514 SOUTH, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. 140 SOUTH HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, INC., A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorney'S Fees And Costs And In Support Of Memo Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 24, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFF 3514 SOUTH, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs. Granted. Moving parties are awarded fees and costs in the amount of $136,315.24. Attorney's fees are permitted under Section 9.1 of the Declaration of Reciprocal Easements, Covenants and Restrictions of 140 South Van Ness pursuant to Civil Code section 1717(a). The requested fees are reasonable. rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 24, 2012, Line 2. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Duplicate entry. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID CHARYN et al VS. NANCY DANN MOORE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 24, 2012, Line 2. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. The City has established that any control over the tree was discretionary. Although Ms. Moore's view may have been obstructed from the limit line, the practice of stopping at a limit line and then "creeping" forward to a point of visibility has long been recognized as "practical" under California law. Substantial evidence supports the reasonableness of the approved design. The design immunity is not lost due to changed conditions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: COALITION FOR ADEQUATE REVIEW VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion To Strike Or In The Alternative To Tax Respondents Coxt Claim; Memo Of P And A; Declaration Of Mary Miles In Support ; Declaration Of Rob Anderson
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 24, 2012, Line 5. PETITIONER COALITION FOR ADEQUATE REVIEW, NINTY-NINE PERCENT AND ROB ANDERSON'S Notice And Motion To Strike Or In The Alternative To Tax Respondents Cost Claim. Granted. The entirety of the City's costs memorandum is stricken. The proceedings on the return are not separate and independent from the proceedings that led up to the return and thus the City is not entitled as a matter of right to costs because it is not a "prevailing party" as that phrase is defined in Code of Civil Procedure section 1032(a)(4). Assuming without deciding that the court has the authority to award costs in its discretion per section 1032(a)(4), the court declines to do so because petitioners were the prevailing party on the initial writ and petitioners, petitioners elected to prepare the record on the return, and an award of costs is fundamentally unfair given that petitioners were never provided with paper copies and the City does not dispute that the electronic version was had little or no utility. Moreover, an award of costs to the City under these circumstances could be viewed as having a deterrent effect on future CEQA petitioners, which is contrary to the enforcement provisions of CEQA, and the City's hourly rates for paralegal time appear to be significantly beyond the actual costs for those timekeepers incurred by the City. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: AON CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. ACTUATE CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 24, 2012, Line 9. DEFENDANT ACTUATE CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained as to all causes of action without leave to amend. All of plaintiff's should have been raised in the federal action and cannot be split into a second lawsuit. The interplay of the 2003 EUSLA and 2005 EUSLA is a key issue in the federal lawsuit. The federal court order denying leave to allege those claims in the federal action is not a basis to file a separate state court action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
